1. anācāra (3. a + ācāra) m.
1〉 ungewöhnliche Erscheinung: pipīlikānācāre  KAUŚ. 93 .
— 2〉 Unsitte: sarvadeśeṣvanācāraḥ pathi tāmbūlacarvaṇam eine Smṛti im  ŚKDR.

results.rendered_html:
anācāra a. Devoid of customary observances or duties, improper in behaviour, unprincipled, regardless of custom, law or propriety &c.; also anācārin in this sense.
— raḥ, -anācaraṇaṃ Absence of due observances or customary duties, improper conduct, departure from established usage or principle; anācāra is of two kinds vihitasya ananuṣṭhānaṃ niṣiddhasya cānuṣṭhānam.
